name: Unplugged Sorting Challenge
description: Students sort themselves or objects by different criteria without using a computer. Explore different sorting strategies: fastest, simplest, most accurate.
learning_objectives: Understand algorithms as sequences of steps
variations: Sort by height, alphabetically, by color; compare algorithms

name: Pattern Detective Game
description: Find and extend patterns in sequences. Develop pattern recognition and ability to generalize rules.

name: Scratch Animation Story
description: Create an animated story using Scratch block-based programming. Use movement, timing, and sound to tell a story.
materials: Ideas for story (fairy tale, original, etc.)
learning_objectives: Use blocks to create movement and interaction
Debug when things don't work as expected

name: Python Number Guessing Game
description: Write a text-based game where the computer picks a number and the player guesses. Use loops, conditionals, and user input.

name: Card Sort Race
description: Sort cards by different algorithms and race them. Compare bubble sort, insertion sort, and merge sort visually.
learning_objectives: Recognize that some algorithms are faster
Analyze why certain algorithms work better

name: Build a Binary Search Tree on Paper
description: Construct a binary search tree by hand, then search it. Understand how data structure choice affects speed.
materials: Optional: cards or nodes to arrange
learning_objectives: See how organization affects search speed

name: Code Review Buddy Exercise
description: Review a peer's code. Look for clarity, efficiency, correctness, and suggestions for improvement. Practice giving and receiving feedback.

name: Git Branching Simulation
description: Simulate version control by creating paper branches, merging changes, and handling conflicts. Understand why version control matters.
materials: Different colored pens for different branches
learning_objectives: See how teams collaborate on code

name: AI Ethics Debate
description: Debate ethical questions about AI and automation. Should we replace human jobs with software? Who's responsible when algorithms make mistakes?

name: Digital Footprint Audit
description: Investigate your digital footprint. What data exists about you online? Who collects it? Is it accurate? How is it used?
materials: Optional: VPN to show IP tracking

name: Fix-the-Code Challenge
description: Given code with bugs, identify and fix the errors. Develop debugging skills in a low-pressure environment.
materials: Code with intentional bugs (Scratch or Python)

name: Data Analysis Project
description: Collect data on something you care about, write a program to analyze it, and create visualizations. Complete cycle: question, collection, analysis, presentation.
materials: Dataset or method to collect data
learning_objectives: Plan and execute a complete project

name: Trace-the-Code Exercise
description: Step through code line-by-line, predicting what happens at each step. Develop understanding before coding.

name: Complexity Analyzer
description: Analyze the time complexity of algorithms. Measure actual runtimes with different input sizes. Predict vs. observe.
learning_objectives: Predict running time for larger inputs

name: Simple Game Creation
description: Create a simple game (Pong, space shooter, or puzzle) with basic game loop, collision detection, and scoring.
materials: Computer with game library (Pygame, p5.js, etc.)

name: Open Source Exploration
description: Explore an open source project on GitHub. Read the code, understand the contribution process, maybe make a small fix.
